discovery.text: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). Istanbul's great pedestrian boulevard — Jacobs appreciates the density of use and the mix of historic arcade buildings with street life.

narrative.summary: Istanbul's great pedestrian boulevard — Jacobs appreciates the density of use and the mix of historic arcade buildings with street life.

blocks.title: Why Jacobs Chose It
blocks.body: 1.4km pedestrian street, historic tram still running down the middle, lined with 19th-century European-style buildings with active arcades and passageways (pasajlar) leading into hidden courtyards. 3 million visitors a day at peak — one of the most intensely used streets in the world.

blocks.title: How to Walk It
blocks.body: Walk the full length. Jacobs is explicit: you have to walk a great street to understand it — the sequence, the proportions, how the edges change. Give it an hour minimum. Sit at a café or on a bench midway. Notice what makes it feel alive.
